result:
Looking at this commission to add an Oculus page for the Parlour apparatus with a real-time chat UI, I'll assess each dimension:

## Test Quality
The tests show strong coverage of the main behavioral contract. The `parlour.test.ts` additions include comprehensive testing of the TurnSummary enrichment with output/costUsd/tokenUsage fields, route filtering logic, and full integration tests using real Hono app instances. The SSE streaming tests manually parse server-sent events and test both success and error scenarios. Tests exercise actual behavior (enriched turn summaries, conversation filtering, streaming chunks) rather than implementation details. Failure paths like missing apparatus, validation errors, and stream interruptions are covered.

## Code Structure  
Well-structured implementation with clear separation of concerns. Route handlers are cleanly decomposed into individual functions in `routes.ts`, with helper functions like `resolveCwd()` properly extracted. The frontend code shows good functional decomposition with clear state management and event handling. The parlour.ts changes integrate cleanly with existing patterns. Abstractions are at appropriate granularity and boundaries are clear - a new contributor could confidently extend this code.

## Error Handling
Consistent error handling across the contribution. Route handlers include proper input validation with 400 responses, try-catch blocks with meaningful SSE error events, and graceful fallbacks when optional apparatus aren't available. Frontend handles network failures, stream cleanup, and missing endpoints gracefully. The streaming implementation properly manages error states and cleanup. Error contexts are informative and allow callers to respond appropriately.

## Codebase Consistency  
Matches existing patterns perfectly at all levels. File organization follows established conventions (`src/routes.ts`, `src/static/`), package.json changes match existing style, and the test structure mirrors other parlour tests. Code style, import patterns, async/await usage, and API design all align with neighboring modules. The vanilla JS frontend approach and CSS custom property usage match the no-build-step Oculus patterns. Reads as if written by the same author as adjacent code.

## Requirement Coverage
All 24 stated requirements are addressed correctly. The implementation includes the LoomApi extensions, TurnSummary enrichment, complete page contribution, all four custom API routes, role/codex selection, conversation management, streaming chat interface, cost tracking, and proper error handling. Edge cases like missing codexes apparatus and lazy conversation creation are handled as specified. The implementation matches both the letter and intent of the specification.

```yaml
dimensions:
  test_quality: 4
  code_structure: 4
  error_handling: 4
  codebase_consistency: 5
  requirement_coverage: 5
composite: 4.4
notes: |
  Strong implementation with comprehensive testing and excellent consistency. 
  The SSE streaming integration tests and vanilla JS frontend demonstrate thorough attention to the requirements.
```
